{"step_by_step_thinking": "Based on the information provided in the documents, post-operative endocarditis can be caused by different bacteria depending on the time interval since valve replacement. Early prosthetic valve endocarditis (within 60 days) is typically caused by S. aureus or coagulase-negative Staphylococcus. Intermediate prosthetic valve endocarditis (between 60 to 365 days) is most commonly caused by Streptococcus or staph infection. Late endocarditis (one year or longer) is typically caused by organisms similar to those found in native valve endocarditis, which are S. viridans and S. aureus. Since the query specifically asks about post-operative endocarditis, we need to consider the time intervals mentioned. Given that the options provided do not include coagulase-negative Staphylococcus, the most likely answer would be Streptococcus viridans for intermediate or late endocarditis.
